activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: The ground is soaked. It must have rained overnight.
activities.explanation: El suelo mojado es evidencia fuerte; «must have rained» expresa una conclusión lógica.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: Nora isn't answering. She might have left her phone at home.
activities.explanation: El teléfono olvidado es una explicación posible, no segura; «might have» es adecuado.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: Jon was abroad all week, so he must have attended Tuesday's local meeting.
activities.explanation: La evidencia descarta la asistencia; debe decir «can't have attended».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: The key isn't here. Alex may have taken it by mistake.
activities.explanation: Sin evidencia concluyente, «may have taken» presenta una explicación posible.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: The lights are off and the door is locked. They must have gone home.
activities.explanation: Dos indicios apoyan una deducción fuerte: «must have gone».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: She must has forgotten the appointment.
activities.explanation: Después del modal usamos «have», no «has»: «must have forgotten».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: The email could have gone to your spam folder; check there first.
activities.explanation: «Could have gone» ofrece una explicación posible y usa el participio correcto «gone».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: The email could have went to your spam folder.
activities.explanation: La estructura exige participio: «could have gone», no «went».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: They only started ten minutes ago, so they can't have finished already.
activities.explanation: El poco tiempo transcurrido permite descartar que hayan terminado: «can't have finished».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: They only started ten minutes ago, so they mustn't have finished already.
activities.explanation: Para una deducción negativa fuerte usamos «can't have», no «mustn't have».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: The package may have been delivered to a neighbour.
activities.explanation: La pasiva «may have been delivered» presenta una ubicación posible del paquete.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: The package may been delivered to a neighbour.
activities.explanation: Falta «have»: la pasiva correcta es «may have been delivered».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: He looks exhausted. He must have been working all night.
activities.explanation: El aspecto cansado apoya la deducción sobre una actividad prolongada: «must have been working».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: The door shows no damage, so the intruder might have used a key.
activities.explanation: La falta de daños hace posible, pero no segura, la explicación de una llave.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: The signature is completely different. Maya can't have signed this form.
activities.explanation: La firma distinta permite descartar que Maya firmara el documento.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: All the windows were closed, so the noise must have come from inside.
activities.explanation: El contexto presenta el interior como la conclusión más lógica; «must have come» es adecuado.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: The figures are inconsistent. Someone could have entered one of them incorrectly.
activities.explanation: Una entrada incorrecta es una explicación posible de la inconsistencia.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: Her coat is still here, so she must have left the building.
activities.explanation: El abrigo sugiere que sigue dentro; la conclusión natural es «can't have left».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: The road was blocked. They may have taken a different route.
activities.explanation: La ruta alternativa es una posibilidad compatible con la carretera bloqueada.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: No one heard the alarm. It could have been switched off before the incident.
activities.explanation: La pasiva modal presenta una explicación posible para que nadie oyera la alarma.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: The server log shows activity at 03.00. An automated process must have been running.
activities.explanation: El registro es evidencia fuerte de una actividad en curso; «must have been running» encaja.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: The archive was sealed until Friday, so the document can't have been removed on Thursday.
activities.explanation: El archivo sellado descarta la retirada; la pasiva «can't have been removed» es precisa.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: The sample was contaminated; the lid might not have been fitted properly.
activities.explanation: Una tapa mal colocada es una causa posible; la pasiva negativa está bien formada.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: Given the identical wording, both reports must have been based on the same source.
activities.explanation: La redacción idéntica apoya una conclusión fuerte expresada en pasiva.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the modal perfect expresses the deduction appropriately.
activities.prompt: The device had no network connection, so it must have uploaded the file remotely.
activities.explanation: Sin conexión, la subida remota es imposible; corresponde «can't have uploaded».
